'''Massimo''' was a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] under the command of [[Mirith Sinn]] and [[Sish Sadeet]] on [[Phaeda/Legends|Phaeda]] following the defeat of the [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor]]. He eventually betrayed Sinn to [[Grappa (Hutt)|Grappa]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], however, and as a "reward" was to be sacrificed to the [[Zanibar]]. The [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guardsman]] [[Kir Kanos]] rescued Mirith Sinn but left Massimo to die. Horribly scarred after Kanos' escape, Massimo swore revenge but was eventually killed by Kanos.

==Biography==
[[File:Massimo1319.jpg|thumb|left|120px|Lt. Massimo.]]

Massimo never wished to join the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]]; he did so out of the necessity of escaping his hidden, troubled past long enough to find a new world to settle on. After being caught up in the war with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], he decided to make the most of his situation.

When the former [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guardsman]] [[Kir Kanos]] found his way to Phaeda and to Sinn via [[Tem Merkon]], Massimo was initially wary of whether they could trust Kanos, whose loyalties were to the deceased [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].

Before Sinn's acceptance of Kanos after he nearly single-handedly saved them from [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Xexus Shev|Shev's]] forces, Massimo threatened to take over Sinn's command. Sinn questioned whether that would make him any different from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] against whom they were fighting.

Although Massimo believed Kanos to be the traitor who informed Shev of their whereabouts, the guilty member was none other than Merkon. When Merkon returned to Sinn's base after secretly meeting with [[Carnor Jax]], Massimo killed him.

Upon the arrival of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] aboard the ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'', Kanos went to [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]], knowing Jax would follow. Sinn and Sadeet also followed, hoping to aid Kanos in his fight against Jax, and Massimo was left to oversee operations on Phaeda.

Aboard the ''Lusankya'', Massimo told Antilles about his previous desire to take over from Sinn and expressed his sincerest apology and newfound loyalty to Sinn and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

When Mirith Sinn infiltrated the court of [[Grappa (Hutt)|Grappa]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] on [[Genon]], however, Massimo betrayed her and the New Republic by revealing to Grappa that Sinn was spying on him. [[Grappa (Hutt)|Grappa]] rewarded Massimo by giving both him and Mirith to [[Zzzanmxl]] and the [[Zanibar]].

The [[Zanibar]] brought Massimo and [[Mirith Sinn]] to the Zanibar homeworld of [[Xo]] where planned on slaughtering the two in a bizarre religious ceremony. Although believed dead, Massimo actually escaped his fate, his prisoner rack having been hit by a stray blaster bolt during Kanos' escape from Zzzanmx. The falling rack knocked Massimo onto a bed of burning coals. Due to his burns and scars, he replaced his right arm and half his face with [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetics]].

Desperate for revenge, he attempted to kill Kanos by luring the former guardsman into a trap, by bribing a magistrate to offer Kanos a fictive bounty on his own head. When Kanos arrived to Massimo's stronghold, he was attacked by the [[Security droid/Legends|security droids]], but managed to evade them and kill Massimo.

==Behind the scenes==
He was voiced by [[Peter Thoemke]] in the [[Crimson Empire (audio drama)|audio drama version]] of ''Crimson Empire''.
